name: Virginia (United States)
description: A deep blue field bearing the obverse of the Great Seal of Virginia — a white circular field with Virtus, in Amazonian dress, standing over a defeated tyrant — encircled by a wreath of Virginia creeper, with the word 'VIRGINIA' above and the motto 'SIC SEMPER TYRANNIS' below.
history: The Great Seal of Virginia was designed in 1776 by a committee that included George Mason, George Wythe, Richard Henry Lee, and Robert Carter Nicholas; the artwork is attributed to George Wythe and the artist Pierre Eugene du Simitiere. The seal was placed on a blue field as a flag during the Civil War; the design was officially adopted as the state flag on 1 February 1950, with technical specifications further codified in later revisions.

features.symbolism: Virtus, the Roman goddess of virtue and the spirit of the Commonwealth, dressed as an Amazon, stands triumphant over a fallen tyrant whose crown lies nearby; the motto 'SIC SEMPER TYRANNIS' ('Thus always to tyrants') appears below; encircled by a wreath of Virginia creeper and the words 'VIRGINIA' above
